epic.spec:
# Web Frontend (FastAPI + Vanilla HTML/JS)

## Overview

Cal-AI is currently CLI-only. The pipeline produces rich structured data (extracted events with AI reasoning, calendar sync results, memory updates) but it's only viewable as console text. Adding a web frontend makes the demo significantly easier to show off — the audience sees pipeline stages animate in real-time via SSE, browses extracted events with confidence badges, and explores the memory system through a dedicated viewer.

**Approach**: FastAPI REST backend serving Jinja2 templates with vanilla JS. No Node.js, no SPA framework. Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream pipeline progress to the browser in real-time. The existing CLI is untouched — the web layer wraps `run_pipeline()` without modifying it.

## Scope

**In scope:**
- `python -m cal_ai serve` subcommand (FastAPI + uvicorn)
- Pipeline page: transcript upload/paste → SSE stage progress → results display
- Memory viewer page: browse stored memories grouped by category
- Dry-run checkbox support
- Docker configuration for web server mode
- Unit tests for web layer

**Out of scope:**
- Authentication / multi-user
- Run history / persistence of past results
- Memory editing (write operations) through the web UI
- Mobile/tablet responsive layout (desktop only)

## Key Decisions (from interview)

### Audience & Style
- **Audience**: Professor/graders only — functionality and clarity over polish
- **Visual style**: Clean & minimal base, but results use a **terminal-style aesthetic** (monospace font, dark background) — consistent across both pages
- **Branding**: Just "Cal-AI" text in the nav bar — no logo, no tagline

### Layout & Navigation
- **Split view layout**: Input panel on left, results panel on right (side-by-side)
- **Navigation**: Two pages only — Pipeline and Memory
- **Stage tracker**: Visual stepper (UI element with dots/spinners/checkmarks), positioned wherever fits cleanest in the results panel
- **Web UI is the primary demo vehicle** — CLI remains as fallback but the web UI is what gets shown

### Pipeline Page — Input
- **Both input methods**: File upload and text paste equally important, tab toggle between them
- **File upload**: Drag-and-drop zone + file picker button
- **File preview**: When a file is uploaded, its contents populate the textarea so the user can review/edit before submitting
- **No file size limit** — it's a demo, accept whatever
- **Textarea**: Fixed reasonable size (~10-15 lines), scrolls for longer content
- **Keyboard shortcut**: Ctrl+Enter submits the form
- **Dry-run checkbox** with clear indicator banner when active

### Pipeline Page — Results (Terminal-Style Output)
- **Terminal aesthetic**: Monospace font, dark background block — looks like watching the pipeline run in a terminal
- **Event display**: Each event shows:
  - CRUD action (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE) with clear sync status (✅ Synced, ⏸️ Dry run, ❌ Failed: reason)
  - Title and readable timestamps (e.g., "Wed Mar 4, 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM")
  - AI reasoning for the action
  - Assumptions shown inline (e.g., "Assumed: duration 1 hour (not specified)")
  - For UPDATE/DELETE: show which existing calendar event was matched
- **Memory updates always shown** in the terminal output, even when zero events are extracted
  - Full detail: action (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE), category, key, value, confidence, reasoning
- **Token/cost info**: Footer of the terminal output (tokens in/out + estimated cost)
- **Summary stats**: Bottom of terminal output (event counts, warnings, duration)
- **Zero events**: Clear message "No calendar events found in this conversation" + memory updates still shown
- **Partial failure**: Failed events shown inline with ❌ indicator and error message

### Pipeline Page — Expandable Log Viewer
- **Real-time log streaming**: Logs appear live as the pipeline runs via SSE (not post-completion)
- **Position**: Collapsed section below the terminal output in the right panel
- **Purpose**: Lets the professor dig into raw pipeline logs if they want to see more detail

### Pipeline Page — State Management
- **Submit button**: Text changes during run (e.g., "Run Pipeline" → "Running...")
- **Concurrent runs**: Inline message "A pipeline is already running. Please wait." near the submit button
- **After completion**: Input clears, results stay visible — user can paste a new transcript immediately
- **SSE disconnect**: Show "Connection lost" error + manual retry option
- **Pipeline crash**: Error shown in the terminal output area, stage tracker shows where it stopped
- **Pipeline speed**: 10-30 seconds typical — stage progress is important feedback

### Pipeline Page — Dry-Run
- **Clear indicator**: Banner or badge saying "DRY RUN — no calendar changes made"
- **Sync status**: Shows "⏸️ Dry run (not synced)" vs "✅ Synced" — distinct from live results

### Missing Configuration
- **Startup warning**: If GEMINI_API_KEY or calendar credentials are missing, server starts but shows a warning banner in the UI

### Memory Page
- **Terminal aesthetic**: Consistent with pipeline page
- **Grouping**: Accordion sections by category (preferences, people, vocabulary, patterns, corrections)
- **Entry detail**: Key + value + confidence indicator per memory entry (skip timestamps, source counts)
- **Empty state**: Instructional message — "No memories yet. Run a pipeline to start building memory about the calendar owner."
- **Refresh behavior**: Always loads fresh data on page load (auto-refresh)

### Browser & Screen
- **Desktop only** — no mobile/tablet responsive needed
- **Browser support**: Chrome + Firefox

## Architecture

```
Browser                    FastAPI                     Pipeline
  |                          |                            |
  |-- POST /api/pipeline/run -->                          |
  |   (multipart: file or text)                           |
  |                          |-- write temp file -------->|
  |                          |-- asyncio.to_thread ------>|
  |                          |   (run_pipeline)           |
  |<-- SSE: stage events ----|<-- logging.Handler --------|
  |<-- SSE: log events ------|<-- logging.Handler --------|
  |<-- SSE: result ----------|<-- PipelineResult ---------|
  |                          |-- cleanup temp file        |
  |                          |                            |
  |-- GET /api/memories ---->|                            |
  |<-- JSON: memories -------|-- MemoryStore.load_all() --|
```

**SSE via log capture**: A custom `logging.Handler` on `cal_ai.pipeline` logger pushes stage messages to an `asyncio.Queue`. The handler filters by thread ID (ensuring isolation). The SSE generator drains the queue via `asyncio.wait_for()` while `run_pipeline()` runs in a background thread via `asyncio.to_thread()`. Zero pipeline code changes.

**Stage event synthesis**: The pipeline logger emits `"Stage 1: ..."` through `"Stage 4: ..."` but does NOT emit `"Stage 1b"` or `"Stage 1c"` lines. The SSE handler synthesizes sub-stage events by matching related log messages:
- Stage 1b (Loading Memories): triggered by `"Memory context loaded"` or `"Memory load failed"` log lines
- Stage 1c (Fetching Calendar): triggered by `"Calendar context fetched"` or `"Calendar context unavailable"` log lines
- **Boundary fallback**: If no 1b/1c log lines appear before `"Stage 2"`, infer them complete at the Stage 2 boundary
- **Terminal rule**: On pipeline completion (result available), mark any still-running stage (especially Stage 4) as complete before emitting `done`. Stage 4 has no following `"Stage 5"` log to trigger its completion.

**Log streaming**: In addition to synthesized stage events, raw pipeline log lines are forwarded as `event: log` SSE events for the expandable log viewer. The log handler captures all messages from `cal_ai.*` loggers (not just stage lines).

**POST-based SSE**: Since `EventSource` only supports GET, the JS client uses `fetch()` + `ReadableStream.getReader()` to parse SSE events from the POST response body.

**Concurrency guard**: An `asyncio.Lock` prevents overlapping pipeline runs. Acquisition uses `asyncio.wait_for(lock.acquire(), timeout=0)` which raises `TimeoutError` → HTTP 409. The lock is released in a `try/finally` block. This atomic pattern avoids the race condition of `if lock.locked(): raise`.

## Quick commands

```bash
# Start web server
python -m cal_ai serve
python -m cal_ai serve --port 8000 -v

# Make targets
make serve
make serve-dev

# Docker
docker compose up  # serves web UI on port 8000

# Run tests
pytest tests/unit/test_web_app.py tests/unit/test_web_schemas.py -v

# Full test suite (existing + new)
make test
```

## New file structure

```
src/cal_ai/web/
  __init__.py          # Exports create_app()
  app.py               # FastAPI app factory, static/template config
  routes.py            # All route handlers (pages + API)
  schemas.py           # Pydantic response models (serialize dataclasses → JSON)
  sse.py               # SSE log capture handler + async generator + stage state machine
  templates/
    base.html          # Base layout (nav, CSS, blocks)
    index.html         # Pipeline page (split view: input left, results right)
    memory.html        # Memory viewer (terminal-style accordion by category)
  static/
    style.css          # All styling (terminal aesthetic, split view, stage tracker)
    app.js             # Pipeline page JS (SSE reader, rendering, drag-and-drop)
    memory.js          # Memory page JS (fetch + render accordion)
```

## Package data configuration

Templates and static assets must be included in the installed package. Add to `pyproject.toml`:
```toml
[tool.setuptools.package-data]
"cal_ai.web" = ["templates/*.html", "static/*.css", "static/*.js"]
```

This ensures `pip install .` (used in Docker) includes non-Python files.

## Key design decisions

- **Temp files for transcript input**: `run_pipeline()` requires a `Path`. Write uploaded/pasted text to a named temp file, pass the path, clean up after. Avoids modifying the core pipeline signature.
- **Raw StreamingResponse**: Use FastAPI's built-in `StreamingResponse` with `media_type="text/event-stream"`. Keeps deps minimal — no sse-starlette needed.
- **Atomic lock acquisition**: `asyncio.wait_for(lock.acquire(), timeout=0)` with `try/finally` release. Avoids racy `if locked()` check.
- **Dependencies in main deps** (not optional extra): This is a demo product — simplicity over flexibility.
- **Pydantic response models**: Mirror `PipelineResult`/`ExtractedEvent`/`EventSyncResult` as Pydantic models with a `from_pipeline_result()` converter. Handles `Path` → `str`, `memory_usage_metadata` → omitted (list of opaque SDK objects), nested `ExtractedEvent` (already Pydantic) → `EventResponse`.
- **Memory API returns flat list**: `GET /api/memories` returns a flat JSON array ordered by category/key. Grouping by category is done client-side in `memory.js`. This matches `MemoryStore.load_all()` which already orders by category/key.
- **Stage synthesis in SSE handler**: Rather than modifying pipeline code, the SSE handler maps log message patterns to synthetic stage events via a state machine. Includes boundary fallback (infer 1b/1c at Stage 2 if no explicit logs) and terminal rule (mark Stage 4 complete on pipeline finish).
- **Uvicorn logging**: `_handle_serve()` passes `log_level` and `log_config=None` to `uvicorn.run()` to preserve the existing `setup_logging()` configuration and honour CLI `-v`.
- **Confidence is categorical**: `ExtractedEvent.confidence` is `"high"|"medium"|"low"` (not numeric). CSS maps these directly to badge colors: high=green, medium=yellow, low=red.
- **Docker CMD-only**: Remove ENTRYPOINT, use CMD-only pattern. Default: serve. CLI override: `docker run cal-ai python -m cal_ai run samples/file.txt`.
- **Terminal-style output**: Results display in monospace dark blocks rather than UI cards — clearer for academic demo, shows raw pipeline intelligence.
- **Split view**: Input stays visible alongside results — user can see transcript context while reviewing what the AI extracted.
- **File preview in textarea**: Drag-and-drop or file picker populates the textarea with file contents for review/edit before submission.
- **Real-time log streaming**: Raw pipeline logs streamed via SSE alongside stage events, displayed in an expandable section below results.
- **Always show memory section**: Memory updates appear in output even when zero calendar events are extracted.
- **Token/cost footer**: Token counts and estimated cost shown at the bottom of the terminal output.

## Edge Cases

- **Empty transcript**: Pipeline extracts zero events → "No calendar events found" message + memory section still shown
- **Partial sync failure**: Some events sync, others fail → failed events shown inline with ❌ and error message
- **Pipeline crash**: Exception → error shown in terminal output area, stage tracker shows where it stopped
- **SSE disconnect**: Connection drops → "Connection lost" message + manual retry option
- **Missing config**: Server starts but shows warning banner for missing API key / calendar credentials
- **Concurrent runs**: Second submit → inline message "A pipeline is already running. Please wait."
- **Empty memory DB**: Memory page shows instructional empty state message
- **No memory log lines**: If no "Memory context loaded" log appears before Stage 2, infer 1b complete at boundary
- **Stage 4 terminal**: Stage 4 has no following stage → mark complete on pipeline finish via terminal rule
- **Large file upload**: No size limit enforced — accept whatever the user uploads (demo context)
- **File + text both provided**: Validation error — require exactly one input method

## Acceptance

- [ ] `python -m cal_ai serve` starts uvicorn on port 8000
- [ ] Browser at `http://localhost:8000` shows split-view pipeline page with upload/paste input on left
- [ ] File upload supports both drag-and-drop and file picker button
- [ ] Uploaded file contents preview in the textarea before submission
- [ ] Ctrl+Enter keyboard shortcut submits the form
- [ ] Submit button text changes during pipeline run (e.g., "Running...")
- [ ] Visual stage stepper shows real-time progress (pending → spinner → checkmark)
- [ ] Terminal-style results show CRUD actions, reasoning, assumptions, sync status, and event matching
- [ ] Memory updates always shown in terminal output with full detail (action, category, key, value, confidence, reasoning)
- [ ] Token/cost info shown as footer of terminal output
- [ ] Summary stats at bottom of terminal output (event counts, warnings, duration)
- [ ] Expandable log viewer below results streams raw logs in real-time
- [ ] Dry-run checkbox produces clear indicator + "would_create" style results
- [ ] Zero-events case shows "No calendar events found" + memory section
- [ ] Partial sync failure shows failed events inline with ❌
- [ ] Pipeline crash shows error in terminal output, stage tracker shows where it stopped
- [ ] SSE disconnect shows "Connection lost" + retry option
- [ ] Missing config shows startup warning banner
- [ ] Concurrent submit shows inline "already running" message
- [ ] `/memory` page shows stored memories in terminal-style accordion sections by category
- [ ] Memory entries show key + value + confidence
- [ ] Empty memory page shows instructional message
- [ ] Memory page auto-refreshes (loads fresh data on page load)
- [ ] `docker compose up` serves the web UI on port 8000
- [ ] Existing CLI (`python -m cal_ai run ...`) still works unchanged
- [ ] All existing tests pass (`make test`)
- [ ] New web tests pass (health, pages, schemas, pipeline mock, memory)
- [ ] Templates/static assets included in `pip install .` package
- [ ] Works in Chrome and Firefox

## References

- `src/cal_ai/pipeline.py:128-393` — `run_pipeline()` and `PipelineResult`
- `src/cal_ai/models/extraction.py:29-60` — `ExtractedEvent` (Pydantic, confidence is categorical)
- `src/cal_ai/pipeline.py:39-120` — `EventSyncResult`, `FailedEvent`, `PipelineResult` (dataclasses)
- `src/cal_ai/pipeline.py:78-120` — `PipelineResult` fields (note: `memory_usage_metadata` is a list, not `usage_metadata`)
- `src/cal_ai/memory/store.py:54-213` — `MemoryStore` (SQLite)
- `src/cal_ai/memory/models.py:22-43` — `MemoryRecord` (Pydantic)
- `src/cal_ai/demo_output.py:32-58` — `format_pipeline_result()` (what data to display)
- `src/cal_ai/__main__.py:31-106` — `build_parser()` (CLI subcommands)
- `src/cal_ai/__main__.py:127` — `known_subcommands` set
- `src/cal_ai/config.py:76-129` — `load_settings()`, `load_memory_settings()`
- `src/cal_ai/pipeline.py:172-391` — Stage log messages (actual patterns verified against source)

tasks.spec:
# fn-10-7x6.1 FastAPI foundation + serve subcommand

## Description
Add FastAPI, uvicorn, Jinja2, and python-multipart as project dependencies. Create the `src/cal_ai/web/` package with an app factory (`create_app()`) that configures Jinja2 templates, static file serving, and a health endpoint. Add a `serve` subcommand to the CLI that starts uvicorn. Add `serve` / `serve-dev` Makefile targets. Add package-data config for templates/static.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `pyproject.toml` — add `fastapi>=0.115`, `uvicorn[standard]>=0.29`, `jinja2>=3.1`, `python-multipart>=0.0.18` to deps; add `httpx>=0.27` to dev deps; add `[tool.setuptools.package-data]` for `cal_ai.web` templates/static
- `src/cal_ai/web/__init__.py` — export `create_app`
- `src/cal_ai/web/app.py` — FastAPI app factory with Jinja2 + StaticFiles config
- `src/cal_ai/web/routes.py` — health endpoint (`GET /api/health`)
- `src/cal_ai/web/templates/.keep` — placeholder so template dir exists in git
- `src/cal_ai/web/static/.keep` — placeholder so static dir exists in git
- `src/cal_ai/__main__.py` — add `serve` subparser (--host, --port, -v), `_handle_serve()`, add `"serve"` to `known_subcommands`, update module docstring
- `Makefile` — add `serve` and `serve-dev` targets
- `tests/unit/test_web_app.py` — health endpoint, static mount, template config

## Approach
- Follow the existing subcommand pattern in `__main__.py:31-106` (`build_parser()`, handler functions)
- Add `"serve"` to the set on `__main__.py:127` (`known_subcommands`)
- Update module docstring at `__main__.py:1-16` to include `serve` subcommand
- Update `build_parser()` docstring to mention `serve`
- App factory pattern: `create_app()` returns configured `FastAPI` instance
- Mount `StaticFiles` on the app (not router) after API routes — avoids path conflict gotcha
- Create `.keep` placeholder files in `templates/` and `static/` dirs so they exist in git (StaticFiles defaults to `check_dir=True` and errors if dir is missing)
- `_handle_serve()` imports uvicorn and calls `uvicorn.run(create_app(), host=..., port=..., log_level=..., log_config=None)`. Pass `log_level="debug" if args.verbose else "info"` and `log_config=None` to preserve existing `setup_logging()` config
- Template dir: `Path(__file__).parent / "templates"`, static dir: `Path(__file__).parent / "static"`
- `[tool.setuptools.package-data]` in pyproject.toml: `"cal_ai.web" = ["templates/*.html", "static/*.css", "static/*.js"]`
- Test with `from fastapi.testclient import TestClient` (Starlette's TestClient, backed by httpx)

## Key context
- StaticFiles must be mounted on `app` object, not an `APIRouter`, and after API routes are included
- StaticFiles defaults to `check_dir=True` — directory must exist or it errors on startup
- `from fastapi.testclient import TestClient` is the correct import (NOT `from httpx`)
- `uvicorn.run()` with `log_config=None` prevents uvicorn from applying its own logging config
- The `monkeypatch_env` and `clean_env` fixtures in `tests/conftest.py` handle env var isolation

## Acceptance
- [ ] `pip install -e ".[dev]"` succeeds with new deps (FastAPI, uvicorn, Jinja2, python-multipart, httpx)
- [ ] `python -m cal_ai serve` starts uvicorn on default port 8000
- [ ] `python -m cal_ai serve --port 9000 --host 127.0.0.1` honours custom host/port
- [ ] `python -m cal_ai serve -v` enables debug logging (uvicorn receives `log_level="debug"`)
- [ ] `GET /api/health` returns `{"status":"ok"}` with 200
- [ ] `make serve` and `make serve-dev` targets work
- [ ] Existing CLI subcommands (run, benchmark, memory) still work unchanged
- [ ] Module docstring and `build_parser()` docstring list `serve` subcommand
- [ ] `pyproject.toml` has `[tool.setuptools.package-data]` for web templates/static
- [ ] `src/cal_ai/web/templates/` and `src/cal_ai/web/static/` dirs exist in git (via .keep)
- [ ] `make test` passes (existing + new tests)
- [ ] `make lint` passes

## Done summary
TBD

## Evidence
- Commits:
- Tests:
- PRs:

data.title: Response schemas + API endpoints (memory + pipeline SSE)

tasks.spec:
# fn-10-7x6.2 Response schemas + API endpoints (memory + pipeline SSE)

## Description
Create Pydantic response models that serialize the pipeline's stdlib dataclasses and nested Pydantic models into clean JSON. Implement the pipeline SSE endpoint (`POST /api/pipeline/run`) and memory JSON endpoint (`GET /api/memories`). The SSE endpoint accepts multipart form data (file upload or pasted text), runs the pipeline in a background thread via `asyncio.to_thread()`, captures stage progress via a custom `logging.Handler` with stage synthesis state machine, and streams SSE events to the client.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `src/cal_ai/web/schemas.py` — Pydantic response models: `EventResponse`, `SyncResultResponse`, `FailedEventResponse`, `PipelineResultResponse`, `MemoryResponse` with `from_pipeline_result()` converter
- `src/cal_ai/web/sse.py` — `PipelineLogCapture(logging.Handler)` with stage state machine + `pipeline_sse_generator()` async generator
- `src/cal_ai/web/routes.py` — expand with `POST /api/pipeline/run` (SSE) and `GET /api/memories`
- `tests/unit/test_web_schemas.py` — schema conversion tests (dataclass→Pydantic, Path serialization, nested models)
- `tests/unit/test_web_app.py` — expand with pipeline SSE mock test, memory endpoint test

## Approach
- Response models mirror fields from `PipelineResult` (`pipeline.py:78-120`), `ExtractedEvent` (`models/extraction.py:29-60`), `EventSyncResult` (`pipeline.py:39-62`), `FailedEvent` (`pipeline.py:65-75`), `MemoryRecord` (`memory/models.py:22-43`)
- `from_pipeline_result(result: PipelineResult)` class method handles:
  - `Path` → `str` (for `transcript_path`)
  - `memory_usage_metadata` → omitted (list of opaque Gemini SDK objects, not JSON-serializable)
  - Nested `ExtractedEvent` (already Pydantic) → `EventResponse`
  - Note: field is `memory_usage_metadata` NOT `usage_metadata`
- **Stage synthesis state machine** in `PipelineLogCapture`:
  - Pipeline only logs `"Stage 1: ..."` through `"Stage 4: ..."` explicitly
  - Sub-stages 1b and 1c do NOT have explicit log lines
  - Handler maps log message patterns to synthetic stage events:
    - `"Stage 1: ..."` → stage 1 running
    - `"Memory context loaded"` or `"Memory load failed"` → stage 1b complete
    - `"Stage 2: ..."` → if 1b/1c not yet emitted, infer them complete (boundary fallback), then stage 2 running
    - `"Calendar context fetched"` or `"Calendar context unavailable"` → stage 1c complete
    - `"Stage 3: ..."` → stage 3 running (stage 2 complete)
    - `"Stage 4: ..."` → stage 4 running (stage 3 complete)
  - **Terminal rule**: On pipeline completion (result available) or `done` event, mark any still-running stage (especially stage 4) as complete before emitting `done`
  - **Empty-memory fallback**: If no memory log line appears before `"Stage 2"`, infer 1b complete at Stage 2 boundary
  - Handler filters by `threading.get_ident()` for thread isolation
  - Pushes to `asyncio.Queue` via `loop.call_soon_threadsafe(queue.put_nowait, ...)`
- **Atomic lock acquisition**: Use `asyncio.wait_for(lock.acquire(), timeout=0)` — catches `TimeoutError` → HTTP 409. Release in `try/finally`.
- Pipeline SSE endpoint: validate exactly one of file/text provided, write to `NamedTemporaryFile`, acquire lock atomically, run `asyncio.to_thread(run_pipeline, ...)`, yield SSE events from queue, cleanup temp file in finally block
- **Memory API returns flat list**: `GET /api/memories` returns flat JSON array of `MemoryResponse` objects ordered by category/key. Client-side grouping in `memory.js`. Uses `load_memory_settings()` → `MemoryStore(path)` → `load_all()` → `close()` pattern from `__main__.py:275-315`.
- Use `StreamingResponse(generator, media_type="text/event-stream")` — no extra SSE deps needed
- Test with `from fastapi.testclient import TestClient` (Starlette's TestClient). Mock `run_pipeline` and have mock emit log lines via `logging.getLogger("cal_ai.pipeline")` so SSE parsing is exercised. Use `client.stream(...)` context manager for SSE tests.

## Key context
- `PipelineResult.transcript_path` is a `Path` object — must serialize to string
- `PipelineResult.memory_usage_metadata` (NOT `usage_metadata`) is a list of opaque Gemini SDK objects — omit from response
- The pipeline logger emits `"Stage 1: ..."` through `"Stage 4: ..."` but NOT `"Stage 1b"` or `"Stage 1c"` — synthesis is required
- Memory-related log lines: `"Memory context loaded..."`, `"Memory load failed..."` (see `pipeline.py:180-195`). Note: there is NO `"No memories found"` line — empty-memory case has no explicit log, so infer 1b complete at Stage 2 boundary
- Calendar-related log lines: `"Calendar context fetched..."`, `"Calendar context unavailable..."` (see `pipeline.py:197-220`)
- `asyncio.Lock` has no atomic try-acquire — must use `wait_for(..., timeout=0)` pattern
- `from fastapi.testclient import TestClient` is the correct import (Starlette's, backed by httpx)
- Stage 4 has no following `"Stage 5"` log — must use terminal rule to mark it complete on pipeline finish

## Acceptance
- [ ] `PipelineResultResponse.from_pipeline_result()` correctly converts all fields including Path→str and nested Pydantic models
- [ ] `memory_usage_metadata` is omitted from the response schema
- [ ] `POST /api/pipeline/run` with file upload returns SSE stream with stage and result events
- [ ] `POST /api/pipeline/run` with text body returns SSE stream identically
- [ ] Providing neither file nor text returns 422 validation error
- [ ] Concurrent pipeline requests return 409 (atomic lock acquisition via `wait_for`)
- [ ] SSE events include synthesized sub-stages: stage 1b (memories) and 1c (calendar context)
- [ ] Stage 4 is marked complete on pipeline finish (terminal rule)
- [ ] Missing 1b/1c log lines result in inferred completion at Stage 2 boundary
- [ ] SSE events include: `event: stage` (with name + status), `event: result` (full JSON), `event: done`
- [ ] Pipeline errors produce `event: error` with message
- [ ] `GET /api/memories` returns flat JSON array of memory records (client groups by category)
- [ ] `GET /api/memories` returns empty array when no memories exist
- [ ] Schema unit tests pass for all model conversions
- [ ] SSE mock test exercises log capture with mock emitting actual log line patterns
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make lint` passes

## Done summary
TBD

## Evidence
- Commits:
- Tests:
- PRs:

# fn-10-7x6.3 Frontend templates + CSS styling

## Description
Create the Jinja2 HTML templates and CSS for both pages. The base template defines shared structure (nav bar, CSS link, JS block). The pipeline page (`index.html`) has the input form (tab toggle between file upload and text paste), stage tracker (vertical progress indicator), and result sections (event cards, sync results, summary). The memory page (`memory.html`) displays memories in accordion sections grouped by category. Add page-serving routes (`GET /`, `GET /memory`).

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `src/cal_ai/web/templates/base.html` — HTML5 base layout: nav bar with Cal-AI branding + page links, CSS link, content block, JS block
- `src/cal_ai/web/templates/index.html` — Pipeline page: input section (upload/paste tabs, dry-run checkbox, submit button), stage tracker (vertical), results section (event cards, sync results, summary)
- `src/cal_ai/web/templates/memory.html` — Memory viewer: category accordions, entry rows (key, value, confidence badge, source count, timestamp), empty state, refresh button
- `src/cal_ai/web/static/style.css` — All styling: CSS custom properties (color scheme), card layouts, confidence badges (green/yellow/red mapped from categorical high/medium/low), stage tracker (pending/running/complete states with spinner), action badges (CREATE=blue, UPDATE=orange, DELETE=red, SKIP=gray), tab toggle, drag-and-drop zone, responsive layout, accordions
- `src/cal_ai/web/routes.py` — add `GET /` and `GET /memory` page routes returning `TemplateResponse`
- `tests/unit/test_web_app.py` — expand with page route tests (200 status, content-type text/html)

## Approach
- Reference `demo_output.py:32-58` (`format_pipeline_result()`) for what data each section displays
- **Stage tracker stages** (6 total, matching synthesized SSE events from Task 2):
  1. "Parsing Transcript" (Stage 1)
  2. "Loading Memories" (Stage 1b — synthesized)
  3. "Fetching Calendar" (Stage 1c — synthesized)
  4. "Extracting Events (LLM)" (Stage 2)
  5. "Syncing Calendar" (Stage 3)
  6. "Updating Memory" (Stage 4)
- Event cards show: title, time range, location, attendees, action badge, confidence badge, expandable reasoning, assumptions list — fields from `ExtractedEvent` (`models/extraction.py:29-60`)
- Sync result rows show: event title + action tag (color-coded) + status — from `EventSyncResult` (`pipeline.py:39-62`)
- Templates use Jinja2 block inheritance: `{% extends "base.html" %}` with `{% block content %}` and `{% block scripts %}`
- All interactive behavior is in JS (Task 4) — templates provide the DOM structure with `id` attributes for JS to target
- No JavaScript in this task — just HTML structure + CSS styling + static rendering
- Remove `.keep` placeholder files from `templates/` and `static/` (created in Task 1) once real files are added

## Key context
- Pipeline page results section is hidden by default (CSS `display: none`), shown by JS after pipeline completes
- Stage tracker items need distinct CSS classes for states: `stage--pending`, `stage--running` (spinner), `stage--complete` (checkmark)
- Stages 1b and 1c are synthesized by the SSE handler (Task 2), not actual pipeline log stages
- **Confidence is categorical** (`"high"`, `"medium"`, `"low"`), NOT numeric — badge CSS maps: `high` = green, `medium` = yellow, `low` = red
- Memory categories from the system: preferences, people, vocabulary, patterns, corrections

## Acceptance
- [ ] `GET /` returns 200 with HTML containing pipeline input form (file upload + text paste tabs)
- [ ] `GET /memory` returns 200 with HTML containing memory viewer structure
- [ ] Base template includes nav bar with links to both pages
- [ ] Stage tracker has 6 stages including synthesized 1b (Memories) and 1c (Calendar)
- [ ] CSS provides distinct visual styles for confidence badges based on categorical values (high=green, medium=yellow, low=red)
- [ ] CSS provides color-coded action badges (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, SKIP)
- [ ] Stage tracker has visual states for pending, running (spinner animation), and complete
- [ ] Layout is responsive (works on desktop and reasonable tablet widths)
- [ ] Pipeline results section is hidden by default
- [ ] Memory page has empty state message
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make lint` passes

# fn-10-7x6.4 Frontend JavaScript (SSE reader + rendering)

## Description
Implement the client-side JavaScript for both pages. The pipeline page JS handles form submission (multipart POST with file or text), reads the SSE stream from the POST response body using `fetch()` + `ReadableStream.getReader()`, updates the stage tracker in real-time, and renders pipeline results (event cards, sync results, summary) into the DOM. The memory page JS fetches the memory API and renders grouped entries.

**Size:** M
**Files:**
- `src/cal_ai/web/static/app.js` — Pipeline page: form submit handler, SSE stream reader (POST-based), stage tracker updater, result renderer (event cards with expandable reasoning, sync results, summary stats)
- `src/cal_ai/web/static/memory.js` — Memory page: fetch `/api/memories`, group by category, render accordion sections with entries

## Approach
- **POST-based SSE**: `EventSource` only supports GET. Use `fetch('/api/pipeline/run', {method:'POST', body: formData})` then `response.body.getReader()` with `TextDecoder` to parse SSE events. This is ~30 lines of JS for the SSE parser.
- SSE parser: accumulate chunks, split on `\n\n`, parse `event:` and `data:` fields
- Stage tracker: on `event: stage` → find DOM element by stage ID, toggle CSS classes (`stage--pending` → `stage--running` → `stage--complete`)
- Stage IDs in SSE events: `"1"`, `"1b"`, `"1c"`, `"2"`, `"3"`, `"4"` — these map to the 6 stage tracker DOM elements from Task 3
- Result rendering: on `event: result` → parse JSON, populate event cards (title, time, location, attendees, confidence badge using categorical `high`/`medium`/`low` values, action badge, reasoning toggle, assumptions), sync result rows, summary counters
- Error handling: on `event: error` → show error alert box; on fetch failure → show network error; on HTTP 409 → show "pipeline already running" message
- Form validation: require exactly one of file or text, disable submit button during pipeline run
- **Memory page**: `fetch('/api/memories')` → response is flat JSON array → group by `category` field client-side → render accordion sections with entry rows (key, value, confidence badge, source count, timestamp)

## Key context
- `ReadableStream.getReader()` returns `Uint8Array` chunks — use `TextDecoder` for streaming decode
- SSE format: each event is `event: <type>\ndata: <json>\n\n` — split buffer on double newlines
- The submit button should be disabled while pipeline is running (re-enabled on `event: done` or error)
- Stage names to display: "Parsing Transcript" (1), "Loading Memories" (1b), "Fetching Calendar" (1c), "Extracting Events (LLM)" (2), "Syncing Calendar" (3), "Updating Memory" (4)
- **Not all 6 stages are guaranteed**: Stages 1b/1c may not appear if memory/calendar steps are skipped or empty. Pipeline can also early-return for zero utterances. Stage tracker must handle partial progression gracefully (leave unvisited stages as pending).
- **Confidence is categorical** (`"high"`, `"medium"`, `"low"`) — map to CSS classes directly, no numeric thresholds
- Event card reasoning section: collapsed by default, click to expand (CSS + JS toggle)
- Memory API returns flat array — JS must group by `category` field

## Acceptance
- [ ] File upload submits correctly and shows SSE stage progress in real-time
- [ ] Text paste submits correctly and shows SSE stage progress identically
- [ ] Stage tracker animates through received stages (pending → running with spinner → complete with check)
- [ ] Stage tracker handles partial progression gracefully (unreceived stages stay pending)
- [ ] Pipeline results display: event cards with title, time, location, attendees, confidence badge (categorical), action badge
- [ ] Event card reasoning is expandable/collapsible
- [ ] Sync results display with color-coded action tags
- [ ] Summary section shows event counts, warnings, duration
- [ ] Pipeline errors show in a visible error alert
- [ ] HTTP 409 (concurrent run) shows appropriate message
- [ ] Submit button is disabled during pipeline execution
- [ ] Form validates that exactly one of file/text is provided
- [ ] Memory page fetches flat API response and groups by category client-side
- [ ] Memory page shows empty state when no memories exist
- [ ] Memory page refresh button works
- [ ] No JavaScript framework dependencies (vanilla JS only)
- [ ] `make lint` passes

# fn-10-7x6.5 Docker + docs update

## Description
Update Docker configuration to default to web server mode and expose port 8000. Update docker-compose.yml with port mapping. Update CLAUDE.md with new web commands, file structure, and architecture notes.

**Size:** S
**Files:**
- `Dockerfile` — add `EXPOSE 8000`, change to CMD-only (remove ENTRYPOINT), set CMD to serve
- `docker-compose.yml` — add `ports: ["8000:8000"]`
- `CLAUDE.md` — add `serve` to Commands section, add web file structure to Project Structure, add `make serve` / `make serve-dev` to commands, document web architecture

## Approach
- Dockerfile: keep existing base image and install steps. Remove ENTRYPOINT, use CMD-only pattern: `CMD ["python", "-m", "cal_ai", "serve", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]`. This allows CLI override via: `docker run cal-ai python -m cal_ai run samples/...`
- Add `EXPOSE 8000` before CMD
- docker-compose.yml: add `ports: ["8000:8000"]` under service — follow existing YAML style
- CLAUDE.md updates:
  - Add `serve` to the subcommands list in the `__main__.py` description
  - Add `src/cal_ai/web/` tree to Project Structure
  - Add `make serve`, `make serve-dev` to Commands section
  - Add `python -m cal_ai serve [--host HOST] [--port PORT] [-v]` to Direct commands
  - Brief note about web architecture (SSE, Jinja2 templates) in Architecture section

## Key context
- Current Dockerfile uses `ENTRYPOINT ["python", "-m", "cal_ai"]` + `CMD ["samples/crud/simple_lunch.txt"]`
- After change: CMD-only. Default runs serve. CLI override: `docker run cal-ai python -m cal_ai run samples/file.txt`
- Package-data config for templates/static is handled in Task 1 (pyproject.toml) — no duplicate work needed

## Acceptance
- [ ] `docker compose up` starts web server on port 8000
- [ ] `docker compose up` web UI is accessible at `http://localhost:8000`
- [ ] `docker run cal-ai python -m cal_ai run samples/crud/simple_lunch.txt` works for CLI use
- [ ] Dockerfile has `EXPOSE 8000`
- [ ] CLAUDE.md documents the `serve` subcommand
- [ ] CLAUDE.md Project Structure includes `src/cal_ai/web/` tree
- [ ] CLAUDE.md Commands section includes `make serve` and `make serve-dev`
- [ ] `make lint` passes
